summary: I re-traced the iteration-2 design against all 34 business requirements, grounding each check in the discovery notes' facts about what the existing Define UI actually surfaces. 32 of 34 requirements are cleanly and defensibly satisfied by the design's explicit R1-R34 traceability table, and there is no meaningful gold-plating (the only unrequested elements are the unused pause_requested_at column and the unpersisted user: params, both trivial, additive, and self-acknowledged as future-audit hooks). The two coverage gaps raised in iteration 1 remain unaddressed and unrebutted in this design. (F1, major) R19 and the requirements' restart-output assumption require a completed 'Repeat from the Beginning' restart to show its fresh, replaced results inline before the person chooses their next action; a restart re-runs the whole Define DAG and regenerates business_requirements (Define's headline deliverable, step 2 of the DAG) in addition to discovery_notes and open_questions, but the design surfaces only the latter two inline (§6.2 adds define_discovery_notes, §6.3 reuses the existing open_questions block, and the R19 traceability row states fresh output is shown 'via R9/R11's same inline blocks'). Per discovery-notes F1, business_requirements has no rendering path in the UI, so this surfacing is net-new work the design does not include, leaving the person to click Done (R20/R21) without ever seeing the regenerated requirements they'd be approving. (F2, minor) R13/R14's 'Ask Human' is wired only as a same-page anchor into the existing questions.present? block; when a paused Define has no current open_questions (e.g. paused right after Explore/Requirements Writer, before the conditional Clarifying-Questions step has produced any — the very region the ask emphasizes), the anchor targets nothing and no answer form renders, leaving R14's free-form-notes-as-feedback path unreachable in that empty state. Neither gap is addressed or rebutted in the design's §9 edge-cases.

findings.issue: R19 and the requirements' restart-output assumption require a completed 'Repeat from the Beginning' restart to show its fresh, replaced results inline in the paused view before the person chooses their next action. A restart re-runs the entire Define DAG and regenerates business_requirements (Define's headline deliverable, produced by Requirements Writer, step 2) alongside discovery_notes and open_questions, but the design surfaces only discovery_notes (new define_discovery_notes helper, §6.2) and open_questions (existing block, §6.3) inline — the R19 traceability row itself states fresh output is shown 'via R9/R11's same inline blocks'. Per discovery-notes F1, business_requirements is surfaced nowhere in the UI today and would need net-new surfacing, which the design does not add. Result: after a restart the person lands on the paused menu and is asked to decide Done (R20/R21) without ever seeing the regenerated requirements inline. Raised in iteration 1; not addressed or rebutted in this design.

R13/R14's 'Ask Human' action is implemented purely as an anchor (#define-ask-human) that scrolls to the existing open-questions/answer form, which renders only when questions.present?. When a paused Define currently has no open_questions (e.g. paused immediately after Explore or Requirements Writer, before the conditional Clarifying-Questions step has run — the region the ask calls out as primary), the Ask Human button targets a non-existent anchor and no answer form is shown, so R14's free-form notes have no submission path in that state. The requirements-completeness critic explicitly deferred this empty-state to the design phase; the design does not resolve it. Raised in iteration 1; not addressed or rebutted in this design.
